rationale: Grohmann (2009) is used in the theory section to define the focal construct of masculine versus feminine/neutral brands, which directly underpins the citing paper's hypotheses. The authors explicitly follow Grohmann's approach to measure brand masculinity and femininity (including trait-based scale items) in their empirical studies, showing methodological reliance. They also cite it to support interpretation that masculinity and femininity are independent brand dimensions. This is clearly more than a passing background citation.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Grohmann (2009) as a core conceptual and measurement foundation for brand gender (masculine/feminine dimensions) and uses those definitions/scales to test new effects of failure versus success messaging. It builds new advertising and emotion-based predictions on top of that established brand-gender framework.
